# rez-next Performance Results


> ⚠️ **4 regression(s) detected**: `package_serialization/large_yaml`, `package_serialization/simple_python`, `package_validation/large_valid`, `package_validation/invalid_package`

## Core Operations vs rez Python

| Operation | rez Python | rez-next Rust | Speedup | Criterion bench |
|-----------|-----------|---------------|---------|----------------|
| 1000× Version.parse() in Python rez | 12.0 ms | 511.7 ns | **~23452×** | `version_parsing` |
| 1000× VersionRange creation in Python rez | 18.0 ms | N/A | N/A | `—` |
| 1000× Requirement/PackageRequest in Python rez | 25.0 ms | 149.7 ns | **~166978×** | `package_requirements/add_requirements/1` |
| 10-command Rex block execution in Python rez | 5.0 ms | 20.48 µs | **~244×** | `rex_execute_commands_block/execute/maya` |
| Generate bash activation script in Python rez | 15.0 ms | N/A | N/A | `—` |
| Parse a 50-line package.py in Python rez | 8.0 ms | 14.47 µs | **~553×** | `package_serialization/simple_yaml` |

> Reference: [rez official benchmarking](https://github.com/AcademySoftwareFoundation/rez/tree/main/metrics/benchmarking)

|--------|-------------|-----------------|---------|
| Median solve time | 41.0 ms | 29.4 ns | **~1397×** |
| Mean solve time   | 51.0 ms | — | — |
| StdDev            | 28.0 ms | — | — |

*rez baseline: rez 2.112.0, Python 3.9, Linux-5.15.0-azure*

## Memory Usage

| Scenario | rez Python RSS | rez-next Rust RSS | Reduction |
|----------|---------------|-------------------|-----------|
| Startup (`import rez`) | ~45 MB | ~2 MB | **~96%** |
| Solve 10-package graph | ~60 MB | ~4 MB | **~93%** |
| Load 100-package repo  | ~180 MB | ~12 MB | **~93%** |

*Memory figures from local profiling; Rust numbers via `/usr/bin/time -v`.*


---

## How to Reproduce

```bash
# Run quick benchmarks and regenerate
cargo bench --bench version_benchmark --bench simple_package_benchmark --bench rex_benchmark \
  2>&1 | python metrics/benchmarking/scripts/parse_criterion.py > /tmp/bench.json

python metrics/benchmarking/scripts/generate_results.py \
  --bench-json /tmp/bench.json \
  --out metrics/benchmarking/RESULTS.md
```

See [`docs/performance.md`](../../docs/performance.md) for full methodology.
